IF MR. NGUYEN FAILS TO COMPLY WITH ALL THREE REQUIREMENTS, THEN HIS ANSWER WILL BE STRICKEN AND PLAINTIFFS MAY REQUEST A PROVE-UP TO OBTAIN A DEFAULT JUDGMENT. IF, HOWEVER, MR. NGUYEN COMPLIES WITH ALL THREE REQUIREMENTS, THEN THE PORTIONS OF THE MOTION SEEKING TO STRIKE MR. NGUYEN'S ANSWER AND EVIDENCE SANCTIONS WILL BE DENIED AND THE WALKERS WILL BE AWARDED FURTHER MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $3000 TO BE PAID NO LATER THAN APRIL 28, 2017 TO COMPENSATE THE WALKERS FOR THEIR FILING OF THIS MOTION. No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER WALKER, ANDREA WALKER Motion To Compel Defendant Bao Nguyen To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents, For Award Of Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant is GRANTED. On November 8, 2016, the Court entered an order granting plaintiff's Motion to Compel, ordering Defendant Nguyen to serve responses to Plaintiffs' Form and Special Interrogatories, Set One, within 14 days and to provide responsive documents by December 1, 2016. Defendant failed to comply with the November 8, 2016 order. Nazarian Decl., par. 9. On January 20, 2017, the Court entered an order requiring Defendant to serve verified code-compliant non-evasive responses by February 2, 2017. On February 2, 2017, Defendant Nguyen served responses (Nazarian Decl., Ex. D). On February 3 and 13, 2017, Plaintiffs' counsel sent two meet and confer efforts regarding the responses. Nazarian Decl., par. 16. The Court finds the responses in the separate statement are not code-compliant. The Court finds that Defendant's counsel failed to meet and confer with plaintiff's counsel on the responses. Defendant is hereby ordered to serve code-complaint, verified responses to the interrogatories listed in the separate statement, within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. Sanctions in the amount of $1,000 are awarded against Defendant Nguyen and in favor of plaintiffs to be paid within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. Plaintiffs' counsel shall serve this order via express mail on all parties. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER WALKER and ANDREA WALKER'S Motion For Order To Strike Defendant Bao Nguyen'S Answer And Enter A Default Judgment Against Defendant; Order For Evidentiary Sanctions; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Bao Nguyen In The Amount Of $7,290.00 is CONTINUED on the court's own motion to February 6, 2017 at 9:30 AM In Department 302. No later than February 2, 2017 defendant Bao Nguyen must: 1) serve verified code-compliant non-evasive responses without any objections except objections based on privilege to all of the discovery referenced in the November 1, 2016 order, 2) pay the sanctions award of $1633, and 3) agree to a continuance of the trial of any length not to exceed six months requested by the Walkers with discovery deadlines to be triggered off the new trial date if requested by the Walkers. If Mr. Nguyen fails to comply with all three requirements, then his answer will be stricken and plaintiffs may request a prove-up to obtain a default judgment. If, however, Mr. Nguyen complies with all three requirements, then the portions of the motion seeking to strike Mr. Nguyen's answer and evidence sanctions will be denied and the Walkers will be awarded further monetary sanctions of $3000 to be paid no later than APRIL 28, 2017 to compensate the Walkers for their filing of this motion. While there is some basis to believe that all of the factual assertions in Mr. Nguyen's opposition papers are not entirely accurate, if Mr. Nguyen complies with three requirements there is no basis to strike his answer or impose evidentiary sanctions against him. Upon stipulation of the parties, the current trial date of February 6, 2017 is ordered CONTINUED to May 22, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 206.
